paragraph: Sovereign Realty Capital completed the acquisition of Thornfield Exchange, a 487,000-square-foot Class A office complex in the Westbridge district of Calderton, for $214.5 million, or approximately $440 per square foot, according to sources familiar with the transaction. The seller, Meridian Crown Partners, had held the asset since 2017, when it acquired the two-tower campus for $161 million. Thornfield Exchange was 91 percent occupied at closing, anchored by a regional headquarters lease with Farwest Financial Group covering 112,000 square feet through March 2034. The deal was financed with a $138 million senior loan from Duncaster Institutional Lenders at a floating rate of SOFR plus 185 basis points, maturing in five years with two one-year extension options. The going-in capitalization rate was reported at 5.4 percent. Sovereign Realty Capital chief investment officer Harrison Velt indicated the firm intends to invest an additional $18 million in lobby modernization, HVAC upgrades, and tenant amenity buildouts over the next 24 months. Eastbridge Realty Advisors represented the seller, while Sovereign Realty Capital was represented internally. The transaction closed on April 22, 2026, and marks Sovereign Realty Capital's fourth acquisition in the Calderton metropolitan statistical area since 2021.

Halverson Property Trust announced a $392 million recapitalization of its Northgate Logistics Portfolio, a collection of eight industrial warehouse facilities totaling 3.1 million square feet spread across the Lakemont industrial corridor in the city of Veldran. The transaction involved the sale of a 49 percent joint venture interest to Penrose Infrastructure Partners, a Luxembourg-domiciled open-end fund, at a blended valuation of $126 per square foot. The portfolio is 98.3 percent leased to fourteen tenants, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 7.2 years. Major occupants include Crestline Distribution Services, Orion Consumer Fulfillment, and Taber Freight Systems. The recap proceeds will be used to retire $175 million in secured debt maturing in August 2026 and to fund a 680,000-square-foot build-to-suit expansion at the Northgate West sub-campus, scheduled for delivery in the fourth quarter of 2027. The capitalization rate implied by the transaction was 4.85 percent. Veldran industrial vacancy stood at 3.9 percent at the time of signing. Cormorant Capital Markets arranged the joint venture equity and facilitated debt placement with Aldwick Life Insurance at a fixed rate of 5.62 percent over ten years.

Pelham Square Hotel Group, a privately held hospitality investor, closed on the $98.7 million purchase of the Ardmore Grand, a 312-key full-service hotel situated at the intersection of Colonnade Boulevard and Fifth Meridian Street in downtown Keswick. The seller, Westmoor Hospitality Ventures, had originally developed the property in 2009 at a cost of $74 million. The Ardmore Grand carries a Courtyard-equivalent flag from a fictional lodging brand, Pinnacle Select Hotels, under a franchise agreement expiring in 2031. The hotel reported trailing twelve-month revenue per available room of $147, a gross operating profit margin of 34.2 percent, and an average daily rate of $189 for fiscal year 2025. Pelham Square financed the acquisition with a $62 million floating-rate bridge loan from Silverton Mortgage Capital, priced at SOFR plus 220 basis points, and approximately $36.7 million in equity. The buyer intends to invest $14 million in a phased renovation of all guest rooms, the ballroom, and the rooftop bar by mid-2027, targeting an ADR of $215 and RevPAR of $168 upon stabilization. Greyfield Hotel Advisors brokered the transaction on behalf of the seller. The deal represents the largest hotel trade in the Keswick CBD since the $104 million sale of the Ridgemoor Suites in 2023.

Aurelia Life Sciences Partners acquired the Oakthorn Research Campus, a 215,000-square-foot life sciences facility in Portsmith's Bayside Innovation District, for $187 million, equating to $870 per square foot. The campus consists of three interconnected laboratory and office buildings, all constructed between 2018 and 2021, with specialized wet-lab infrastructure including 14-foot clear heights, 15 watts per square foot of power capacity, and enhanced HVAC systems meeting ASHRAE 90.1 standards. The seller, Inland Coast Opportunity Partners, acquired the land in 2015 and delivered the first building in 2018. At closing, the campus was 94 percent leased to six tenants, led by Varex Genomics, which occupies 88,000 square feet under a lease running through December 2030. The going-in cap rate was 4.6 percent. Financing consisted of a $110 million ten-year fixed-rate mortgage from Chandler National Life at 5.85 percent and a $32 million mezzanine tranche provided by Quartus Credit Partners at an all-in rate of 8.4 percent. Aurelia intends to develop an adjacent 90,000-square-foot shell building on a 1.8-acre land parcel included in the sale, with construction anticipated to begin in the first quarter of 2027. Stonebridge Property Advisors represented the seller.

Caldwell Retail Opportunity Trust, a non-traded REIT focused on necessity-based retail, completed the $45.3 million purchase of Fernwood Village Center, a 198,000-square-foot grocery-anchored shopping center located in the Fernwood submarket of Orrenton. The center is anchored by a 54,000-square-foot Garfield Foods supermarket operating under a lease expiring in 2036, with eight-percent rent bumps every five years. Additional tenants include a national pharmacy, a regional fitness operator occupying 22,000 square feet, and eighteen inline merchants. Total center occupancy at closing stood at 96.2 percent, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 5.8 years. The transaction was financed with a $28.5 million ten-year fixed-rate loan from Portside Savings Bank at 5.45 percent interest only for the first five years. The going-in capitalization rate was 6.15 percent. Caldwell Retail Opportunity Trust's portfolio now encompasses 34 properties totaling 4.7 million square feet in 11 states. The transaction was sourced off-market through a long-standing relationship with the seller, Hillborn Retail Ventures. Delray Property Brokers acted as transaction advisor to the seller, and the deal closed on March 15, 2026.

Glenmore Data Facilities, a developer and operator of hyperscale data centers, secured a $275 million construction loan from a syndicate led by Rivergate Commercial Bank and Northcliff Institutional Lending to fund development of the Halcyon Data Hub, a 240,000-square-foot, 60-megawatt campus situated on a 34-acre site in the Southfield Technology Corridor of Maplewood. The facility is pre-leased at 100 percent capacity to two hyperscale cloud tenants under 15-year triple-net leases with annual rent escalations of 2.5 percent, generating estimated stabilized net operating income of $31.4 million annually. Construction is slated to begin in June 2026, with shell delivery expected in the third quarter of 2027 and tenant fit-out completion in early 2028. The loan carries a floating rate of SOFR plus 165 basis points, with a 36-month initial term and two 12-month extensions. Glenmore's equity contribution totals $118 million, sourced from a capital raise completed in February 2026 at a 6.2 percent preferred return. Maplewood's Southfield corridor currently hosts 18 operational data center facilities with an aggregate capacity of 1.4 gigawatts. Meridian Debt Advisors arranged the construction syndication.

Stratford Residential REIT completed the $321 million acquisition of the Elmwood Crossings multifamily portfolio, comprising four garden-style apartment communities totaling 1,842 units located in the northern suburbs of Halcrest. The portfolio was assembled by Dunmore Capital Group between 2014 and 2019 at a total cost of approximately $198 million. The four communities range in age from seven to twelve years, with a combined average unit size of 882 square feet and an average monthly rent of $1,385 per unit at closing. Portfolio-wide occupancy was 95.1 percent at the time of sale. The transaction implies a per-unit price of $174,268 and a going-in cap rate of 5.05 percent. Stratford Residential REIT assumed $147 million in existing agency debt, comprising two Fandon Mae and one Fredmont Mac loans, with a blended interest rate of 3.89 percent and a remaining weighted average term of 6.4 years. Additional financing consisted of a $52 million mezzanine loan from Crestview Alternative Credit at 7.8 percent. Stratford indicated plans to invest $28 million across the portfolio in kitchen and bathroom unit upgrades, with targeted rent premiums of $150 to $220 per month upon renovation. NHM Realty Advisors represented the seller.

Vantage Industrial Partners closed a $68.4 million refinancing of the Broadmoor Logistics Center, a 612,000-square-foot single-tenant distribution facility located at 4400 Meridian Commerce Parkway in the East Portway industrial submarket of Dunford. The property is net-leased to Crestgate Supply Chain Solutions under a 12-year agreement, with 7.5 years of term remaining and 2.0 percent annual rent escalations. The loan, provided by Arcadian Life Assurance, carries a fixed interest rate of 5.38 percent on a ten-year term with 30-year amortization. Loan-to-value at origination is 58 percent, based on a third-party appraisal of $118 million. The refinancing replaces a $44.5 million floating-rate construction loan from 2021. Proceeds include $12 million in cash-out, which Vantage intends to deploy toward land acquisition for a planned 400,000-square-foot speculative industrial facility in an adjacent submarket. The Broadmoor Logistics Center features 36-foot clear heights, 78 dock-high doors, four drive-in ramps, and a 185-foot truck court. Current net operating income is approximately $6.3 million annually. Dunford's East Portway submarket vacancy rate was 4.1 percent at the time of refinancing, according to Cornhill Property Research.

Prestige Office Partners executed a 99-year ground lease on the 1.7-acre Harwick Parcel in downtown Thornbury, providing the foundation for a planned 620,000-square-foot mixed-use tower combining Class A office space, ground-floor retail, and a 180-key boutique hotel. The land is owned by the Thornbury Municipal Pension Authority, which will receive an initial annual ground rent of $4.2 million, subject to CPI-linked adjustments every five years capped at 2.5 percent per annum. Prestige Office Partners secured $445 million in construction financing led by Cortland Investment Bank and Kessler Mortgage Trust, with the loan bearing interest at SOFR plus 210 basis points. Total project cost is estimated at $690 million, with a projected stabilized yield on cost of 6.8 percent. The office component spans 420,000 square feet, the hotel 148,000 square feet, and retail accounts for 52,000 square feet across the ground and second floors. Prestige has secured a pre-lease commitment from Whitmore Consulting Partners covering 95,000 square feet of office space for 12 years. Groundbreaking is targeted for September 2026, with delivery scheduled for the third quarter of 2029. Westpoint Realty Finance advised Prestige on the ground-lease structure.

Kestrel Realty Income Trust, a publicly traded net-lease REIT with a market capitalization of approximately $3.8 billion, announced the acquisition of a 24-property single-tenant portfolio for $312.6 million, reflecting a blended going-in cap rate of 5.7 percent. The portfolio encompasses retail, quick-service restaurant, and automotive service assets across 14 states, with a weighted average lease term of 11.3 years and annual rent escalations averaging 1.8 percent. All leases are absolute triple-net, with no landlord maintenance obligations. The properties range in size from 2,200 to 8,900 square feet, with an average site size of approximately 1.4 acres. The seller, Meridian Net Lease Fund III, a closed-end fund managed by Meridian Capital Advisors, had assembled the portfolio through individual acquisitions between 2019 and 2023. Financing consisted of $195 million drawn on Kestrel Realty Income Trust's revolving credit facility at a current rate of 5.15 percent. Closing occurred on April 30, 2026. The acquisition increases Kestrel's total portfolio to 1,247 properties in 39 states, with aggregate annualized base rent of $218 million. Kestrel's board approved a $0.042 per share increase to the monthly dividend coincident with the announcement.

Ironwood Capital Group completed the sale of the Harborview Plaza office tower, a 38-story, 775,000-square-foot Class A high-rise in the Central Business District of Portcliffe, for $589 million, equating to $760 per square foot. The buyer, Arcadia Global Investors, a sovereign wealth-adjacent fund, acquired the asset as part of a broader strategy to expand holdings in major United States gateway office markets. Ironwood Capital Group had acquired the tower in 2016 for $412 million and subsequently invested $47 million in common area modernization and sustainability upgrades, including a LEED Platinum recertification completed in 2022. At closing, Harborview Plaza was 88 percent leased to 21 tenants, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 6.9 years and annual gross rent of approximately $36 million. The going-in cap rate was 5.15 percent. Arcadia Global Investors assumed $280 million in existing CMBS debt, priced at a fixed rate of 4.22 percent maturing in January 2029. Ironwood's internal rate of return on the investment was reported at approximately 14.7 percent. Greystone Property Advisors and Corum Realty Services jointly represented the seller, while Arcadia was advised by Halverson Realty Capital.

Dunmore Realty Corp completed a $134 million construction-to-permanent loan financing of Clearwater Commons, a 389-unit Class A multifamily community under development at 9200 Clearwater Drive in the Riverside submarket of Fenwick. The loan was originated by Ternion Life Insurance Company at a fixed rate of 5.72 percent on a 10-year term, with a 30-year amortization schedule commencing after a 36-month interest-only period. The development spans four six-story buildings on a 6.4-acre site, featuring a mix of studio, one-, two-, and three-bedroom units averaging 834 square feet, with projected market rents ranging from $1,490 to $2,680 per month. Total project costs are estimated at $198 million, inclusive of land, hard construction, and soft costs. Dunmore Realty Corp's equity contribution is $64 million. The community is expected to deliver its first units in February 2027, with stabilization targeted at 95 percent occupancy by the fourth quarter of 2027, implying a stabilized net operating income of $9.1 million and a 4.6 percent yield on cost. Amenities include a resort-style pool deck, coworking lounge, EV charging stations, and a 12,000-square-foot fitness facility. Northgate Real Estate Capital arranged the permanent financing.

Aldgate Industrial Partners announced the 100 percent leasing of Aldgate Industrial Park, Phase II, a speculative 1.24-million-square-foot industrial development completed in November 2025 in the Southbrook Commerce Zone of Radford. The final two available units, totaling 318,000 square feet, were leased to Carmody Packaging Solutions and Trellis Retail Logistics on triple-net terms of 10 and 8 years, respectively. Blended base rent for the final leases averaged $9.85 per square foot, with 3 percent annual escalations. Phase II is anchored by a 600,000-square-foot lease to Meridian Fulfillment Group signed prior to groundbreaking in early 2024. Total development cost for Phase II was $112 million, implying a stabilized yield on cost of 7.1 percent based on projected net operating income of $7.95 million. The project features 40-foot clear heights, ESFR sprinkler systems, and a fully secured truck court accommodating 102 trailers. Aldgate Industrial Partners is now advancing Phase III, a 1.8-million-square-foot expansion on an adjacent 95-acre parcel, with site work expected to begin in August 2026. Redfern Industrial Brokerage represented tenants in both final transactions.

Portsmouth Value-Add Fund IV, managed by Cornerstone Real Estate Advisors, acquired the Eastgate Business Park, a 14-building suburban office campus totaling 820,000 square feet in the Eastgate submarket of Coverton, for $71.8 million, or $87.56 per square foot. The seller, a regional family office, had owned the campus since 1998. At acquisition, the park was 67 percent occupied, with in-place net operating income of approximately $4.1 million, implying an acquisition cap rate of 5.7 percent on current income. Cornerstone's business plan calls for converting the four lowest-occupied buildings, totaling 248,000 square feet, to flexible life sciences lab suites and creative office space, an effort budgeted at $38 million over 24 months. The remaining 572,000-square-foot conventional office component is slated for leasing at market rents of $22 to $28 per square foot, full service. Financing was provided by Belfort Commercial Lending in the form of a $48 million floating-rate bridge loan at SOFR plus 275 basis points with a 36-month initial term. Cornerstone anticipates a stabilized value of $155 million upon completion of the conversion and occupancy normalization. Meridian Office Advisors handled the transaction on behalf of the seller.

Westmoor Mortgage Trust, a commercial mortgage REIT, originated a $178 million senior mortgage loan secured by the Mariner's Wharf mixed-use development in the Harborside neighborhood of Saltport. The collateral consists of a 410-unit luxury multifamily tower and 32,000 square feet of ground-floor retail space, all delivered in January 2026. The loan carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 195 basis points with a two-year initial term and three one-year extension options, subject to performance hurdles. The multifamily component was 78 percent leased at loan origination, with average effective rents of $2,340 per month across studio, one-, and two-bedroom configurations. The retail component is 65 percent leased, with four signed leases to food-and-beverage and fitness tenants. As-stabilized appraised value was reported at $297 million, implying a loan-to-value ratio of 59.9 percent at origination. The borrower, Harborside Realty Development, is a local developer that broke ground on the project in March 2023. Westmoor Mortgage Trust's chief credit officer noted that the Saltport apartment market recorded year-over-year effective rent growth of 4.2 percent in 2025, supporting the underwriting thesis. Galloway Capital Markets arranged the financing.

Pemberton Square Realty Trust, a publicly traded diversified REIT, announced the disposition of the Crestview Tech Center, a four-building, 565,000-square-foot suburban office campus in the Crestview submarket of Alderton, for $101.5 million, or $179.65 per square foot. The buyer, a private equity fund advised by Thornton Value Investors, acquired the campus with plans for a conversion to last-mile logistics and light industrial use. Pemberton Square Realty Trust acquired the Crestview Tech Center in 2014 for $187 million and had absorbed declining occupancy since the pandemic, with in-place occupancy at closing of 58 percent. The sale price implies a current cap rate of 4.1 percent on in-place income and a significant write-down from book value. Pemberton Square Realty Trust will record an impairment charge of approximately $61 million in the second quarter of 2026. Proceeds will be redeployed into industrial and data center acquisitions consistent with the company's stated portfolio repositioning strategy. The transaction closed on May 7, 2026. Corum Realty Advisors and Alderton Property Services jointly arranged the sale. The disposition reduces Pemberton Square's office allocation from 28 percent to 22 percent of total portfolio net asset value.

Cascade Realty Partners closed the acquisition of the Whitmore Industrial Gateway, a newly constructed 2.05-million-square-foot industrial park located on a 148-acre site in the exurban Eastbrook submarket of Vernondale, for $231 million, or approximately $112.68 per square foot. The seller, Ridgeline Development Group, delivered the project in two phases between Q3 2024 and Q1 2026. The park is fully leased to three tenants — Northfield Consumer Logistics, Vantage Global Distribution, and Elmore Building Products — under triple-net leases with remaining terms of 10, 12, and 8 years, respectively. Blended base rent at closing is $9.40 per square foot on a NNN basis, with 2.5 percent annual escalations. The going-in cap rate was 5.3 percent, implying in-place net operating income of approximately $12.2 million. The transaction was financed with a $148 million ten-year fixed-rate loan from Cornwater Life Insurance at 5.48 percent on a 30-year amortization schedule. Cascade's equity contributed $83 million through its third closed-end fund, Cascade Industrial Fund III. The Vernondale Eastbrook submarket recorded 6.4 million square feet of net absorption in 2025, with vacancy remaining below 3 percent. Meridian Industrial Advisors and Westport Capital Markets jointly brokered the transaction.

Halcyon Residential Capital completed a $415 million preferred equity recapitalization of the Greenvale Towers Portfolio, a collection of three luxury high-rise apartment buildings totaling 1,104 units in the Greenvale neighborhood of Caldport. The preferred equity was placed with Monarch Sovereign Fund, a Korean pension-linked vehicle, at an 8-percent preferred return with a five-year term. The portfolio, developed and owned by Greenvale Residential Partners since 2019, was appraised at $695 million, reflecting average per-unit values of approximately $630,000. All three towers are stabilized, with a portfolio-wide occupancy rate of 96.7 percent and average in-place rents of $3,480 per month per unit. Annual net operating income is approximately $32.4 million, implying an overall going-in cap rate of 4.66 percent on the total appraised value. The recapitalization proceeds were used to retire $340 million in existing construction and bridge debt, with the residual equity distributed to the sponsor. Greenvale Residential Partners retains a promoted interest in the venture and continues as operating partner. Northgate Real Estate Capital structured the preferred equity transaction and advised Greenvale Residential Partners throughout the process.

Trellis Realty Group executed the $53.8 million sale-leaseback of the Dunmore Corporate Center, a 198,000-square-foot single-tenant suburban office building located at 11200 Commerce Boulevard in the Dunmore submarket of Fairvale. The seller-lessee, Alderton Manufacturing Holdings, will continue to occupy the entirety of the building under a 15-year absolute triple-net lease at an initial rent of $16.50 per square foot, with 2-percent annual bumps. The going-in cap rate for the buyer, Kestrel Net Lease Income Fund, was 6.05 percent. Alderton Manufacturing Holdings intends to redeploy the $53.8 million in proceeds toward capital expenditures at two production facilities. The building, constructed in 2008, was most recently appraised at $56 million, suggesting a modest discount to appraised value. The facility features 24,000 square feet of executive office space, a 500-seat cafeteria, a 15,000-square-foot training center, and 820 surface parking spaces. Kestrel Net Lease Income Fund financed the acquisition with a $33 million ten-year fixed-rate mortgage from Greenfield Savings Association at a rate of 5.55 percent. Redfern Corporate Solutions represented Alderton Manufacturing Holdings in the transaction, and Vantage Net Lease Advisors acted for Kestrel.

Ridgemont Office Partners and its institutional equity partner, Torchlight Capital Partners, announced the ground-up development of the Parkway Centre, a 380,000-square-foot Class A office building at 500 Innovation Parkway in the Northgate Business District of Cedarholm. Total project costs are estimated at $248 million, equating to $652 per square foot, with a targeted stabilized yield on cost of 7.2 percent and an estimated stabilized net operating income of $17.8 million. The project is being financed with a $164 million construction loan from a consortium of three regional banks, priced at SOFR plus 195 basis points over a 30-month term. Ridgemont and Torchlight have secured pre-lease commitments totaling 142,000 square feet, or 37 percent of the building, from two professional services firms: Kellner Advisory Group and Westbridge Litigation Support. Office asking rents in the Northgate Business District range from $38 to $46 per square foot on a full-service basis, among the highest in the Cedarholm metropolitan area. Groundbreaking is scheduled for July 2026, with shell completion targeted in the first quarter of 2028. Corning Realty Services is handling leasing assignments, with Thornwall Architecture and Eastbrook Builders serving as design and construction partners.

Brightwater REIT, a publicly traded healthcare and medical office real estate investment trust, completed the $164.5 million acquisition of the Harmon Medical Pavilion, a 322,000-square-foot, four-building medical office campus adjacent to Harmon Regional Hospital in the Westport submarket of Alderton. The seller, Sunridge Health Properties, divested the campus as part of a strategic portfolio pruning initiative. Brightwater REIT is acquiring the asset at a 5.8 percent going-in cap rate, based on in-place net operating income of $9.54 million. The campus is 97.2 percent leased to seven physician groups, a regional hospital system, and two specialty outpatient surgery operators, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 8.3 years. Brightwater financed the acquisition with a $98 million ten-year fixed-rate mortgage from Pelham National Life at 5.65 percent and $66.5 million in equity sourced from its operating line. Post-closing, Brightwater REIT's total portfolio comprises 118 properties totaling 9.6 million square feet, with an aggregate gross asset value exceeding $2.8 billion. Halcrest Realty Advisors represented the seller. The transaction closed on April 9, 2026.

Cormorant Capital Advisors, on behalf of its Opportunity Zone Fund II, executed the acquisition of the former Westlake Printing Works site, a 4.2-acre urban industrial property at 880 Westlake Avenue in the Ironside neighborhood of Halborn, for $21.4 million. The transaction enables Cormorant to develop a 310-unit mixed-income residential tower on the brownfield parcel, with 20 percent of units reserved for households earning below 80 percent of area median income, a condition required for Opportunity Zone basis step-up benefits. Total development cost is projected at $147 million, inclusive of site remediation estimated at $6.8 million. The project will be financed with a $98 million construction loan from Duncastle Community Bank and $49 million in Opportunity Zone equity from 16 individual and institutional investors. Cormorant anticipates breaking ground in December 2026 and delivering the first residential units in the second half of 2028. The 42-story tower will offer studio through three-bedroom units ranging from 485 to 1,240 square feet, with market-rate rents estimated at $2,100 to $3,600 per month. Ironside's residential vacancy rate stood at 2.8 percent as of March 2026, among the tightest in the Halborn metropolitan area.

Northfield Realty Capital disclosed that its fourth non-traded REIT, Northfield Income Properties IV, completed the acquisition of Rivergate Commerce Center, a 1.05-million-square-foot multi-tenant industrial park in the South River submarket of Fordham, for $118 million, or $112.38 per square foot. The 11-building park was sold by Meridian Industrial Holdings following a seven-year hold during which Meridian invested $12.4 million in roof replacements, LED lighting retrofits, and dock-leveler upgrades. The park was 93.5 percent occupied at sale to 23 tenants, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 3.8 years and an average in-place rent of $8.40 per square foot, estimated to be 12 percent below market. The going-in cap rate was 5.95 percent, based on in-place net operating income of $7.03 million. Northfield Income Properties IV financed the acquisition with a $74 million floating-rate loan from Greyfield Commercial Mortgage at SOFR plus 205 basis points. Northfield intends to mark-to-market leases upon rollover, targeting a stabilized rent of $9.45 per square foot and net operating income of approximately $8.4 million at full stabilization. Westmont Industrial Brokerage and Velden Realty Group jointly brokered the sale.

Langfield Self-Storage Partners announced the $88.6 million acquisition of a six-property self-storage portfolio comprising 4,340 units and approximately 512,000 net rentable square feet in the greater Millbrook metropolitan area. The seller, Fenwick Storage Group, assembled the portfolio between 2012 and 2020, with three facilities in suburban Millbrook, two in the adjacent community of Cresthaven, and one in Elmore Township. Portfolio-wide occupancy at closing was 91.3 percent, with an average effective street rate of $146 per unit per month. The going-in cap rate was 5.4 percent, based on in-place net operating income of $4.78 million. Langfield financed the acquisition with $57 million in financing provided by Portside Mortgage Corporation at a fixed rate of 5.62 percent over ten years with a 30-year amortization. The acquisition is consistent with Langfield's strategy of acquiring mid-size, suburban self-storage portfolios in markets with below-average new supply pipelines. The six properties are concentrated along the Route 74 corridor, which Langfield views as a supply-constrained submarket benefiting from strong household formation trends. Millbrook's self-storage vacancy rate stood at 6.8 percent as of year-end 2025. Clearwater Storage Advisors facilitated the transaction.

Fenbrook Real Estate Fund VI, a value-add commingled fund managed by Fenbrook Capital Partners, closed a $92.4 million acquisition of the Copperfield Retail Center, a 425,000-square-foot power center located at the intersection of Copperfield Boulevard and Highway 62 in Sandton. The center is shadow-anchored by a national electronics retailer in a separately owned pad, with in-line tenants including a home improvement chain, a furniture retailer, two sporting goods chains, and multiple junior anchor tenants. Occupancy at acquisition was 81 percent, with 80,500 square feet of vacant space across four anchor bays. The going-in cap rate was 7.8 percent on occupied income. Fenbrook's business plan involves backfilling vacant boxes with medical office users and entertainment tenants, categories showing the strongest demand in the Sandton retail market. Fenbrook financed the transaction with a $58 million bridge loan from Redfern Bridge Capital at SOFR plus 305 basis points and $34.4 million in fund equity. The seller, Copperfield Partners LLC, a family-controlled operator, had held the asset since 2006. Targeted unlevered internal rate of return for the investment is 11.5 to 13 percent over a five-year hold. Eastgate Realty Advisors brokered the sale.

Aldwick Realty Capital Trust, a publicly traded diversified REIT, completed a $500 million unsecured notes offering, with $250 million of 5.45 percent notes due 2031 and $250 million of 5.90 percent notes due 2036. Net proceeds will be used to repay $375 million in floating-rate secured debt and to fund near-term property acquisitions. Following the offering, Aldwick Realty Capital Trust's weighted average interest rate declines from 5.61 percent to 5.49 percent, and its weighted average debt maturity extends from 4.2 years to 5.8 years. The company's total capitalization is approximately $9.4 billion, with a debt-to-total-enterprise-value ratio of 38 percent. Aldwick's portfolio comprises 224 properties totaling 28.7 million square feet, spanning industrial, office, and retail asset classes. The offering was led by Greyfield Investment Bank, Calderton Securities, and Vantage Capital Markets. The notes are rated investment grade at BBB/Baa2 by the two applicable rating agencies. Aldwick's board reaffirmed 2026 guidance for funds from operations of $3.18 to $3.24 per diluted share, inclusive of the incremental interest cost savings from the debt restructuring. The offering closed on May 12, 2026.

Harwick Real Estate Partners completed the sale of the Harwick Commerce District, a 78-acre master-planned industrial park in the Harwick Township submarket of Trentville, in a portfolio disposition valued at $343 million. The buyer, Meridian Industrial Core Fund, an open-end commingled vehicle with $8.2 billion in assets under management, acquired all 14 buildings totaling 3.05 million square feet at a blended price of $112 per square foot. The park, constructed in phases between 2016 and 2024, is 97.5 percent occupied to 32 tenants with a weighted average remaining lease term of 5.4 years and average in-place rents of $8.65 per square foot. The going-in cap rate was reported at 5.05 percent, based on net operating income of approximately $17.3 million. Meridian Industrial Core Fund will assume $180 million in existing secured debt at a blended fixed rate of 4.34 percent, with the remainder of the purchase price funded through the fund's unlevered equity capital. Harwick Real Estate Partners achieved an unlevered internal rate of return of 16.2 percent across the portfolio over the multi-phase hold period. Redfern Industrial Advisors acted as exclusive broker on behalf of the seller. The transaction closed in two tranches, with final closing on May 5, 2026.

Veramont Mortgage Partners originated a $47 million bridge loan secured by the Lakeshore Apartments, a 224-unit workforce housing community located at 5800 Lakeshore Drive in the Ridgepoint submarket of Millhaven. The loan carries a floating rate of SOFR plus 250 basis points over an initial 24-month term with one 12-month extension option. The borrower, Clearbrook Residential Development, acquired the community for $56 million in January 2025 and is executing a unit interior renovation program targeting 180 of the 224 units, with projected rent premiums of $130 to $175 per month post-renovation. Physical occupancy at origination was 89 percent, with average in-place rents of $1,210 per month. Based on an as-stabilized appraisal of $79 million and assuming full renovation completion, the loan-to-stabilized-value ratio is 59.5 percent. The Lakeshore Apartments were originally constructed in 1987 and received a partial exterior renovation in 2014. The Ridgepoint submarket has recorded consistent rent growth of 5.1 percent per year over the last three years, according to Millhaven Apartment Research. Clearbrook Residential intends to refinance into agency debt upon achieving stabilization. Pelham Capital Markets arranged the bridge financing.

Cornerstone Net Lease Trust executed the $31.7 million acquisition of a 14,800-square-foot freestanding retail property located at 4401 Meridian Drive in the retail corridor of Lakewood Junction, fully leased to a nationally recognized pharmacy chain under an absolute NNN lease expiring in October 2034. The lease includes three five-year renewal options exercisable at the tenant's discretion, with a flat rent structure providing no escalations. Current annual base rent is $1.92 million, equating to a going-in cap rate of 6.06 percent. The tenant's S&P credit rating is investment grade at BBB+. The property was constructed in 2009 and is located on a 1.8-acre outparcel in front of a regional supermarket center, providing strong co-tenancy and traffic drivers. Cornerstone Net Lease Trust funded the acquisition through a combination of proceeds from its continuous equity offering and borrowings on its $275 million revolving credit facility. The transaction was sourced by Cornerstone's internal acquisitions team and required no broker engagement. The Lakewood Junction retail corridor reported an overall vacancy rate of 4.2 percent as of Q1 2026. The acquisition is the 88th property in Cornerstone's growing net-lease portfolio, which now spans 27 states.

Duncastle Property Trust, a publicly traded office and mixed-use REIT, announced the recapitalization of the Duncastle Innovation Quarter, a 2.3-million-square-foot urban campus in the Midway neighborhood of Clearwater that was originally developed by the trust between 2010 and 2019. The transaction involved the formation of a joint venture with Longshore Sovereign Partners, which acquired a 45 percent interest in the campus for $487 million, implying a total asset valuation of approximately $1.08 billion, or $470 per square foot. The campus is 84 percent leased to a diversified mix of technology, life sciences, and professional services tenants, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 5.8 years and average in-place rents of $42.50 per square foot on a full-service gross basis. Net operating income is approximately $41.5 million, reflecting a 3.84 percent capitalization rate on the total implied value. Duncastle Property Trust will retain a 55 percent interest and continue as operating partner, with management fees of 1.25 percent of effective gross income. Proceeds to Duncastle total approximately $440 million, after retirement of $253 million in secured debt encumbering three of the five campus buildings. Westbrook Capital Advisory structured the joint venture recapitalization.

Morningfield Capital Partners acquired the Grandview Marriott-flagged full-service hotel, a 428-key property located at 100 Convention Center Drive in downtown Alderton, for $121.5 million, or approximately $284,000 per key. The property was sold by a regional pension fund that had owned the hotel since 2007 as part of a diversified real estate portfolio. The hotel achieved a trailing 12-month revenue per available room of $158, an average daily rate of $209, and an occupancy rate of 75.6 percent for calendar year 2025. Gross operating profit margin was 31.8 percent, generating house profit of approximately $9.7 million. The going-in capitalization rate, based on net operating income after a five-percent management fee reserve and capital replacement reserve of four percent of revenue, was 5.6 percent. Morningfield Capital financed the acquisition with a $78 million floating-rate loan from Aldwick Commercial Mortgage at SOFR plus 235 basis points, and $43.5 million in equity. The buyer intends to invest $18 million in a comprehensive guestroom renovation and lobby overhaul during 2027, targeting a post-renovation ADR of $235 and RevPAR of $180. Convention Center Drive Realty Advisors brokered the transaction.

Silvertop Real Estate Capital announced that Silvertop Opportunity Fund III acquired the Crestwick Office Portfolio, a collection of five suburban office buildings totaling 610,000 square feet across three submarkets of Brockmore, for $58.2 million, or $95.41 per square foot. The portfolio was offered by Clearmont Properties, which had struggled to maintain occupancy following pandemic-era lease expirations. Aggregate occupancy at acquisition was 64 percent, generating in-place net operating income of approximately $3.7 million. Silvertop's business plan calls for repositioning two of the five buildings as co-working and flexible office space, converting one building to residential use through an entitlement process expected to take 18 to 24 months, and leasing the remaining two buildings to conventional office tenants at revised market rents of $21 to $24 per square foot. Total planned capital expenditure is $28 million over the three-year investment horizon. Financing was provided by Redfern Bridge Capital in the form of a $38 million floating-rate loan at SOFR plus 290 basis points. Silvertop targets an equity multiple of 2.1x and an unlevered internal rate of return of 12 to 14 percent. Brockmore Office Advisors handled the competitive sale process.

Pelham Real Estate Debt Fund V originated a $95 million first-mortgage bridge loan secured by Meridian Station, a 402-unit multifamily community located at 2600 Station Boulevard in the Midtown submarket of Grenville. The borrower, Grenville Residential Partners, acquired the stabilized community for $139 million, or $345,771 per unit, from a closed-end opportunistic fund at a going-in cap rate of 5.3 percent. The loan carries a floating rate of SOFR plus 210 basis points, with a two-year initial term and two one-year extension options. Grenville Residential Partners intends to execute a $15 million capital improvement plan focused on amenity upgrades, unit interior renovations on 180 units, and exterior landscaping improvements. Average in-place rents at the time of acquisition were $1,820 per month, estimated at 8 percent below market. The property was delivered in 2017 and is currently 96.8 percent occupied. Pelham Real Estate Debt Fund V's loan represents a 68.3 percent loan-to-cost ratio. The Grenville multifamily market has sustained rent growth averaging 4.8 percent annually for the past three years, according to Meridian Apartment Analytics. Whitmore Capital Markets arranged the financing.

Trellis Commerce Trust, a specialty industrial REIT, completed the acquisition of Trellis Cold Storage Center, a 540,000-square-foot temperature-controlled warehouse facility in the Northhaven industrial submarket of Dunford, for $148.5 million, or $275 per square foot, reflecting the premium associated with specialized cold storage infrastructure. The facility, developed by Harwick Cold Chain Partners in 2021 at a cost of $118 million, includes 420,000 square feet of refrigerated storage across four temperature zones ranging from -20 degrees Fahrenheit to 38 degrees Fahrenheit, along with 120,000 square feet of dry warehouse and processing space. The property is 100 percent leased to Meridian Fresh Logistics under a 15-year triple-net lease, with two-percent annual rent escalations and a current annual rent of $8.4 million. The going-in cap rate was 5.65 percent. Financing consisted of a $95 million ten-year fixed-rate mortgage from Galveston Life Insurance at 5.55 percent. Trellis Commerce Trust estimates a replacement cost premium of more than 30 percent relative to the acquisition price, providing significant downside protection. Cold storage market vacancy in Dunford stood at 1.8 percent as of Q1 2026. Westport Industrial Advisors exclusively represented the seller.

Corning Realty Trust, a non-traded perpetual-life REIT focused on industrial and logistics real estate, acquired the Sycamore Industrial Collection, a portfolio of nine infill last-mile distribution facilities totaling 975,000 square feet in the urban core of Halborn, for $212 million, or $217.44 per square foot. The assets, ranging in size from 48,000 to 165,000 square feet, are located within five miles of the Halborn central business district and serve dense population clusters with estimated 30-minute delivery reach of 2.4 million households. The portfolio is 99 percent leased to 17 tenants, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 4.1 years and average in-place rents of $17.20 per square foot, estimated 11 percent below current market rents of approximately $19.25 per square foot for comparable infill product. The going-in cap rate was 5.15 percent. Corning Realty Trust financed the acquisition with a $130 million secured credit facility from Aldwick National Bank at SOFR plus 175 basis points. The seller, Halborn Infill Industrial Partners, an affiliate of Riverside Opportunity Advisors, had assembled the portfolio through off-market acquisitions from 2018 to 2023. Fernbrook Capital Markets advised the seller.

Brightfield Property Group successfully completed the development and full lease-up of the Brightfield Tech Quarter, a 520,000-square-foot Class A office and research campus in the Millhaven Innovation District, achieving 100 percent occupancy two months ahead of schedule. The five-story campus, constructed over 28 months at a total cost of $218 million, or $419 per square foot, includes 480,000 square feet of office space and 40,000 square feet of amenity and retail uses. Anchor tenants include Vantage Analytics Corporation, which signed a 12-year lease for 180,000 square feet in 2023 prior to groundbreaking, and Westbridge Biotech Holdings, which executed a 10-year lease for 115,000 square feet in 2024. The remaining tenancy is distributed among six additional firms in technology, professional services, and financial services. Effective rents average $39.50 per square foot on a full-service basis. Based on estimated stabilized net operating income of $16.4 million, the project's yield on cost is 7.52 percent. Brightfield is marketing the campus for sale or recapitalization, with initial price guidance in the range of $310 to $330 million, implying a going-in cap rate of 5.0 to 5.3 percent. Greystone Property Markets is running the formal disposition process.

Halcrest Realty Partners, acting through its Halcrest Stabilized Income Fund II, acquired a portfolio of three Class B multifamily communities totaling 742 units in the suburban Westport and Ferndale neighborhoods of Kellerton for $97.6 million, or $131,537 per unit. The seller, a private family partnership, had owned the communities for between 18 and 24 years. At closing, portfolio-wide occupancy was 93.2 percent, with average in-place rents of $1,105 per month, estimated at 16 percent below market. The three properties range from 196 to 312 units and were originally constructed between 1984 and 1992. Halcrest's renovation plan targets 85 percent of units for kitchen, bathroom, and flooring upgrades, with budgeted costs of $9,800 per unit and expected rent premiums of $145 to $190 per month post-renovation. Total renovation budget is $6.2 million over a 24-month period. The going-in cap rate was 6.2 percent on in-place income. Financing was secured from Windshore Federal Savings in the form of two fixed-rate agency-style loans totaling $63 million at a blended rate of 5.41 percent, with 10-year terms and 30-year amortization. Kellerton Residential Advisors brokered the sale process.

Sageworth Data Ventures announced the execution of a 20-year ground lease with the Alderton Port Authority on a 22-acre industrial waterfront parcel at 1 Harbor Commerce Drive in the Portside submarket of Alderton, enabling the development of a 150,000-square-foot edge computing and carrier-neutral data center facility with an aggregate power capacity of 40 megawatts. Annual ground rent is $2.1 million, subject to CPI adjustments every five years capped at 3 percent. Total development cost is estimated at $310 million, inclusive of $180 million in data center-specific mechanical and electrical infrastructure. Sageworth has secured pre-commitment agreements from four network operators and two cloud service providers covering 28 megawatts of critical load capacity at a blended rate of $210 per kilowatt per month on 10-year contracts. The project is being financed with a $195 million construction loan from Crestmont Infrastructure Lending and $115 million in equity from Sageworth's flagship digital infrastructure fund. Construction is expected to begin in August 2026, with Phase I delivery of 20 megawatts targeted in the fourth quarter of 2027. Duncastle Real Estate Advisors advised the Port Authority on the ground lease negotiation.

Meridian Equity Partners and co-investor Westbrook Institutional Fund completed the $178 million acquisition of the Fairlawn Promenade, a 495,000-square-foot open-air lifestyle center in the affluent Fairlawn submarket of Greenvale. The seller, Corning Retail Ventures, had owned the center since its development in 2005. Fairlawn Promenade is anchored by a 62,000-square-foot specialty grocer and a 35,000-square-foot health and wellness operator, with 65 additional tenants including a curated mix of regional and national specialty retailers, restaurants, and personal services providers. Occupancy at closing was 95.8 percent, and inline sales productivity averaged $540 per square foot for the trailing 12 months ended December 2025, well above the national average for comparable lifestyle centers. In-place net operating income is approximately $10.4 million, reflecting a going-in cap rate of 5.84 percent. Financing consisted of a $112 million ten-year fixed-rate loan from Pemberton Life at 5.42 percent. Meridian and Westbrook plan a $14.5 million refresh of the center's common areas, landscaping, signage, and seasonal programming infrastructure over the first 24 months of ownership. Eastgate Retail Advisors exclusively represented the seller.

Thornbury Industrial Fund II, a closed-end value-add fund managed by Thornbury Capital Group, secured a $41 million senior mortgage refinancing of the Elmwood Distribution Hub, a 298,000-square-foot multi-tenant industrial park in the Elmwood submarket of Portcliffe. The loan, originated by Duncastle Community Savings at a fixed rate of 5.68 percent over seven years on a 25-year amortization schedule, replaces a $29 million floating-rate construction loan from 2022. The park was developed by Thornbury and completed in phases through early 2024, with full lease-up achieved in September 2025. Current occupancy is 100 percent across nine tenants, with in-place rents averaging $10.20 per square foot and a weighted average remaining lease term of 5.6 years. Based on a stabilized net operating income of $2.85 million and a third-party appraised value of $72.5 million, the new loan represents a 56.6 percent loan-to-value ratio. The refinancing generates $9.8 million in cash-out proceeds for Thornbury Industrial Fund II, to be redeployed into a planned speculative 185,000-square-foot industrial facility on an adjacent two-acre land parcel acquired in 2023. Whitmore Commercial Mortgage arranged the refinancing.

Elmore Realty Trust, a publicly traded net-lease REIT focused on essential retail, announced the completion of its disposition of 35 properties for total proceeds of $187.3 million, reflecting a blended exit cap rate of 5.9 percent. The assets, comprising 22 quick-service restaurant properties, nine automotive service centers, and four convenience stores with fuel, were sold across three separate portfolio transactions to three different buyers over a 60-day marketing period. Weighted average remaining lease term across the divested portfolio was 7.4 years. Net proceeds, after satisfaction of $102 million in property-level mortgage debt, total approximately $85 million. Elmore Realty Trust will reinvest proceeds into higher-growth metropolitan markets with stronger rent growth profiles and longer lease durations. Following the dispositions, Elmore's portfolio totals 1,108 properties in 36 states, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 9.8 years and an average going-in cap rate on recent acquisitions of 5.65 percent. The company reaffirmed full-year 2026 adjusted funds from operations guidance of $1.84 to $1.90 per diluted share. Redfern Net Lease Advisors ran the marketing process for all three portfolio transactions.

Crestview Residential Partners announced the $67.3 million preferred equity investment by Argent Institutional Partners in the Crestview Summit, a 482-unit luxury multifamily high-rise under construction at 2400 Summit Drive in the Ridgecrest neighborhood of Portsmith. The preferred equity carries an 8.5 percent current pay return plus a 1.5 percent accrual, with a projected three-year term aligned to the anticipated stabilization and refinancing of the development. Total project cost is $238 million, funded through a combination of a $148 million construction loan from Riverton Commercial Bank at SOFR plus 175 basis points, the $67.3 million in preferred equity, and $22.7 million in common equity contributed by Crestview Residential Partners. The 42-story tower is scheduled to deliver its first units in May 2027 and achieve 95-percent occupancy by late 2027. Projected market rents range from $2,400 for studios to $5,800 for three-bedroom penthouse units, with an average effective rent of $3,050 per unit. The project includes ground-floor retail totaling 4,800 square feet and below-grade parking for 420 vehicles. Portsmith's Ridgecrest submarket absorbed 1,640 new apartment units in 2025, with market occupancy remaining at 95.1 percent. Pelham Structured Capital advised Crestview on the preferred equity placement.

Dunmore Capital Trust announced the $249 million refinancing of the Dunmore Tower, a 45-story, 960,000-square-foot Class A office skyscraper in the Central Business District of Calderton. The new financing, originated by a three-lender syndicate comprising Aldwick National Bank, Riverton Life Insurance, and Greyfield Mortgage Trust, replaces $198 million in existing CMBS debt that was approaching its final maturity date. The loan carries a fixed all-in rate of 5.74 percent over a seven-year term with a 30-year amortization schedule commencing after three years of interest-only payments. Loan proceeds include $44 million in cash-out, net of transaction costs. The tower is 91 percent leased to 18 tenants, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 6.2 years and effective gross annual income of approximately $48 million. The refinancing was based on an independent appraisal of $430 million, implying a loan-to-value ratio of 57.9 percent. Since acquiring the tower in 2014 for $312 million, Dunmore Capital Trust has invested $38 million in capital improvements including elevator modernization, lobby renovation, and the addition of a tenant conference center and fitness facility. Westbridge Commercial Finance arranged the refinancing.

Lakemont Property Partners completed the sale of the Lakemont Corporate Center, a 16-acre, five-building suburban office campus totaling 437,000 square feet in the Lakemont Business Park submarket of Grenville, for $38.5 million, or $88.10 per square foot. The buyer, Meridian Adaptive Realty Partners, plans to convert the campus to a healthcare and medical office use through a phased redevelopment. At sale, the campus was 52 percent occupied, with in-place rents of $19.50 per square foot, full service. The going-in cap rate on occupied income was 5.2 percent, with an all-in acquisition cap rate of approximately 2.7 percent reflecting the vacancy drag. Meridian intends to invest $58 million over three years to upgrade mechanical systems, add surgery suites, increase parking, and subdivide floor plates to accommodate medical tenants. Target stabilized rent upon redevelopment completion is $28 per square foot on a full-service basis across the converted buildings, with projected stabilized net operating income of $5.4 million. Meridian financed the acquisition and redevelopment program with a $62 million construction and renovation loan from Portside Commercial Lending. Cornhill Property Advisors and Halcrest Realty Services co-brokered the transaction.

Falconer Development Partners broke ground on the Westgate Commerce Center, a three-phase, 4.5-million-square-foot speculative industrial development on a 295-acre greenfield site in the Westgate Township submarket of Veldran. Phase I encompasses 1.2 million square feet in three separate buildings ranging from 300,000 to 480,000 square feet, all featuring 40-foot clear heights, ESFR sprinkler systems, cross-dock configurations, and 200-foot truck courts. Total Phase I development cost is projected at $98.4 million, with an anticipated yield on cost of 7.0 percent based on current market rents of $8.65 per square foot. Construction financing was arranged with Aldwick Regional Bank in the form of a $65 million floating-rate loan at SOFR plus 185 basis points over a 24-month term. The Veldran market absorbed 8.2 million square feet of industrial space in 2025, with vacancy declining to 4.7 percent. Falconer anticipates initiating lease negotiations as soon as framing is complete, targeting a combination of single-tenant buildouts and multi-tenant configurations for Phase I. Phases II and III are contingent on Phase I lease-up performance, with Phase II planned for 1.6 million square feet and Phase III for 1.7 million square feet. Cornhill Industrial Leasing is handling all Phase I marketing assignments.

Eastbrook REIT, a publicly traded diversified real estate investment trust, executed a 1031 exchange transaction, disposing of the Eastbrook Office Plaza, a 215,000-square-foot suburban office building in Alderton, for $29.8 million, and simultaneously acquiring the Thornfield Logistics Hub, a newly constructed 680,000-square-foot industrial facility in the Thornfield submarket of Veldran, for $81.4 million. The two transactions were structured to qualify as a like-kind exchange under Section 1031 of the Internal Revenue Code. The office property was sold at a cap rate of 7.4 percent and was 76 percent occupied at closing, while the industrial acquisition reflects a going-in cap rate of 5.35 percent. The Thornfield Logistics Hub is fully leased to a single tenant, Meridian Distribution Services, under a 10-year NNN lease at a rent of $8.65 per square foot with three-percent annual escalations. The balance of the industrial acquisition, $51.6 million, was funded with a new $53 million mortgage from Rivergate Life Insurance at a fixed rate of 5.48 percent. The exchange transaction was structured by Langfield 1031 Exchange Services and facilitated by Halborn Property Advisors on the industrial side and Corum Realty Services on the office disposal.

Clearbrook Realty Capital completed the disposition of a 90 percent equity interest in the Clearbrook Pinnacle, a 324-unit luxury multifamily tower in the Pinnacle District of Halborn, to Sovereign Realty Income Fund, an open-end perpetual-life vehicle, for $261 million, valuing the full asset at $290 million, or approximately $895,062 per unit. Clearbrook developed the 36-story tower, which was delivered in January 2024, at a total cost of $248 million. The community achieved 95-percent occupancy within 14 months of opening and reported average effective rents of $3,620 per month for the trailing 12-month period ended March 2026. Net operating income for 2025 was $13.1 million, reflecting a stabilized cap rate of 4.52 percent on the total asset value. Sovereign Realty Income Fund financed its equity acquisition with a combination of fund equity and a $155 million ten-year fixed-rate mortgage from Aldwick National Life at 5.62 percent. Clearbrook will retain a 10-percent interest in the joint venture and continue as property manager for a fee of 3.5 percent of effective gross income. Fernbrook Realty Advisors represented Clearbrook in the recapitalization process.

Greyfield Industrial Partners secured entitlements for the Greyfield Crossroads Logistics Park, a planned 2.8-million-square-foot speculative industrial campus on a 186-acre site at the junction of Interstate 72 and State Route 44 in the Crossroads Township submarket of Millbrook. The project will be developed in three phases of approximately 930,000 square feet each, with Phase I construction financing being arranged through Westmoor Commercial Bank in the form of a $76 million floating-rate construction loan at SOFR plus 175 basis points. Total Phase I development cost is budgeted at $108 million, implying a construction cost of $116 per square foot. Projected Phase I yield on cost at current market rents of $9.15 per square foot, triple-net, is 7.25 percent. Phase I will consist of two buildings: a 580,000-square-foot single-tenant cross-dock building and a 350,000-square-foot multi-tenant flex distribution building. The Millbrook Crossroads submarket has experienced consistent rent growth of 6.1 percent annually over the past four years, driven by limited land supply and strong e-commerce demand. Phase I construction is scheduled to begin in October 2026, with delivery expected in the fourth quarter of 2027. Redfern Industrial Leasing is handling pre-leasing assignments.

Aldgate Real Estate Securities, a commercial real estate structured finance vehicle, closed a $340 million CMBS conduit transaction backed by 18 commercial real estate loans originated across eight states. The pool includes eight retail properties, six industrial facilities, two multifamily communities, one hotel, and one mixed-use asset. The aggregate outstanding principal balance of the collateral pool is $340 million, with a weighted average loan-to-value ratio of 61.3 percent, a weighted average debt service coverage ratio of 1.72x, and a weighted average going-in cap rate of 5.85 percent. The transaction was structured in six tranches, with the senior AAA-rated class representing $231 million priced at a spread of 72 basis points over the comparable-maturity swap rate. Subordinate tranches were placed with a mix of B-piece buyers, insurance accounts, and CMBS-focused hedge funds. The pool's weighted average interest rate is 5.96 percent, and the weighted average remaining loan term is 8.7 years. Corning Investment Bank and Westbrook Capital Markets co-managed the securitization. Aldgate Real Estate Securities plans to issue a subsequent CMBS conduit in the third quarter of 2026, targeting a pool balance of $420 million. Rating agencies Westmark Ratings and Cordate Analytics assigned ratings to all six tranches.

Thornwall Realty Trust, a publicly traded healthcare-focused REIT, entered into a definitive agreement to acquire a seven-property senior housing portfolio comprising 892 independent living, assisted living, and memory care units in the southeastern United States, for total consideration of $218 million, or $244,395 per unit. The seller, Ridgepoint Senior Housing Ventures, operated the properties under a RIDEA structure with a third-party management company, Sunrise Eldercare Services. The portfolio reported net operating income of $9.8 million for the trailing 12 months ended December 2025, reflecting a going-in cap rate of 4.49 percent. Portfolio-wide occupancy was 88.4 percent at the time of signing, with targeted stabilized occupancy of 93 percent within 18 months following the acquisition. Thornwall Realty Trust intends to retain Sunrise Eldercare Services as manager under a new five-year management agreement with a 5.5-percent management fee. The acquisition will be funded with a combination of $140 million in proceeds from a concurrent equity offering and $78 million drawn on Thornwall's $600 million unsecured revolving credit facility. The transaction is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026, subject to customary regulatory approvals. Westbridge Healthcare Realty Advisors represented the seller.
